skill.name: gemini-computer-use
skill.description: Build and run Gemini 2.5 Computer Use browser-control agents with Playwright. The skill provides setup guidance and a Python agent loop for screenshot-based browser automation.
skill.summary: Run a Gemini Computer Use browser automation loop with Playwright.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Markdown backticks, weak cryptography, and certificate files are false positives after context review. The confirmed risk is legitimate but elevated: the Python script sends browser screenshots and URLs to Gemini, then executes model-selected browser actions through Playwright.

medium_findings.title: Browser Screenshots Sent to External AI Service
medium_findings.description: The script captures browser screenshots and sends them with the user prompt to the Gemini API. This is required for the skill, but it can expose sensitive page content, account data, or private URLs if used on authenticated sites.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly captures screenshots and includes them in model requests and function responses. The behavior is legitimate for Computer Use, but privacy exposure is clear.

medium_findings.title: Model-Directed Browser Actions
medium_findings.description: The script executes Gemini function calls as Playwright actions, including navigation, clicking, typing, key combinations, scrolling, and drag operations. This can affect websites or accounts when the browser is authenticated.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed action list and dispatcher show direct execution of model-selected browser actions. Legitimate automation use is possible, but the action surface is broad.

medium_findings.title: Safety Confirmation Depends on Model Signal
medium_findings.description: The script prompts for confirmation only when the model includes a safety_decision that requires confirmation. There is no independent local policy check for risky clicks, typed text, downloads, purchases, or account changes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The confirmation path is implemented, but it is gated by a model-provided field. I found no local classifier or rule set that independently blocks sensitive browser actions.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access Is Expected
low_findings.description: The static API key findings are true for environment access but not evidence of hardcoded or stolen credentials. The sample env file contains empty placeholders, and the script uses GEMINI_API_KEY to initialize the Google client.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code reads an API key from the environment and exits if it is missing. No evidence found of secret logging, hardcoded credentials, or unexpected exfiltration beyond the configured Gemini API use.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick findings in documentation are Markdown formatting or ordinary setup commands. They do not indicate hidden runtime code execution inside the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced code blocks or inline Markdown references. No evidence found that these Markdown backticks execute automatically.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Certificate Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography and certificate or key-file detections do not match cryptographic operations. The flagged script lines are argument descriptions, mouse coordinates, and keyboard actions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain no hash function, cipher selection, certificate parsing, or private key material. The detections appear to be keyword collisions.

low_findings.title: Configurable Browser Executable Needs User Trust
low_findings.description: The script can launch a browser executable path provided through COMPUTER_USE_BROWSER_EXECUTABLE. This is useful for custom Chromium browsers, but users should avoid sourcing untrusted env files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The executable path is environment-controlled and then passed to Playwright launch options. This is an opt-in feature, not evidence of malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked Agent Loop With Screenshots
dangerous_patterns.description: Browser screenshots and current URLs are packaged into Gemini function responses. Users should treat pages opened during a run as data shared with an external AI provider.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The screenshot capture and API request construction are explicit. The risk is data exposure rather than hidden malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automated UI Control From Model Output
dangerous_patterns.description: Model function calls are converted into Playwright mouse, keyboard, navigation, and scroll operations. This is the core feature and should be used in a constrained browser profile.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dispatcher maps model action names directly to browser operations. The implementation includes action allowlisting but still grants broad UI control.

content.value_statement: Browser automation agents are hard to wire together safely and consistently. This skill provides setup steps and a Playwright loop for Gemini Computer Use workflows.
actual_capabilities: Creates a runnable Python agent loop for Gemini Computer Use.
Uses Playwright to open Chromium, navigate pages, and execute browser actions.
Sends screenshots and function responses back to the Gemini model.
Supports safety confirmation when the model requests user approval.
Allows users to exclude predefined Computer Use actions at runtime.
Documents environment setup for API keys and browser selection.
limitations: Requires a valid Gemini API key and Google GenAI access.
Shares browser screenshots and URLs with the Gemini service during runs.
Does not provide an independent local safety policy for risky actions.
Targets browser automation only, not desktop-wide computer control.

use_cases.title: Prototype Browser Agents
use_cases.description: Build a working proof of concept that lets Gemini inspect pages and operate a Chromium browser.

use_cases.title: Test Computer Use Workflows
use_cases.description: Run controlled browser tasks and inspect how screenshots, function calls, and responses move through the loop.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Safety Controls
use_cases.description: Experiment with excluded actions, confirmation prompts, and sandboxed browser profiles before broader deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Simple Browser Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a basic web navigation task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to run Gemini Computer Use against a public website. Start at [URL], complete [task], and use no more than [turn count] turns.

prompt_templates.title: Restrict Risky Actions
prompt_templates.scenario: Limit what the browser agent can do during a run.
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure the Computer Use agent for [goal]. Exclude these actions: [actions]. Explain how the exclusions change the browser behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt the Agent Loop
prompt_templates.scenario: Modify the script for a project workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the Playwright agent loop for [project need]. Preserve screenshot feedback, function responses, and safety confirmation handling.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Deployment Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before running on sensitive sites.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my plan to run this Computer Use agent on [site type]. Identify privacy risks, browser sandbox settings, excluded actions, and human approval points.

output_examples.input: Run a browser task that finds the latest public blog post title on a website.
output_examples.output: A short terminal session plan with environment setup, the start URL, the prompt, and the turn limit to run the agent.

output_examples.input: Restrict the agent so it cannot type text or use keyboard shortcuts.
output_examples.output: A recommended command configuration and an explanation that typing and key-combination actions will be unavailable to the model.

output_examples.input: Assess whether the agent can be used on an authenticated admin page.
output_examples.output: A risk-focused checklist covering screenshot sharing, account permissions, sandbox profiles, excluded actions, and required human confirmation.

best_practices: Run the browser in a fresh profile with the minimum account permissions needed.
Use excluded actions for typing, navigation, or clicks that are not required for the task.
Avoid pages with secrets, customer data, payments, or irreversible account actions.
anti_patterns: Do not run the agent inside a browser profile that is logged into sensitive accounts by default.
Do not source env files from untrusted repositories or shared folders.
Do not treat model safety confirmation as a complete policy control.

faq.question: Does this skill include runnable code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Python script that runs a Gemini Computer Use loop through Playwright.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The script expects GEMINI_API_KEY in the environment before it runs.

faq.question: Does it send browser data outside my machine?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends prompts, screenshots, and current page context to the Gemini API.

faq.question: Can it control a logged-in browser session?
faq.answer: It can operate any browser context you give it. Use a separate profile with limited access.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because the browser automation and screenshot sharing are expected but sensitive.
